heading: Grants for young witness assistance
label: 20143 Grants for young witness assistance
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 3743 of Title 42

Amendments2006—Subsec. (a). substituted “The Director of the Bureau of Justice Assistance of the Office of Justice Programs may” for “The Attorney General, acting through the Bureau of Justice Assistance, may”.Pub. L. 109–271
amendments: Amendments2006—Subsec. (a). substituted “The Director of the Bureau of Justice Assistance of the Office of Justice Programs may” for “The Attorney General, acting through the Bureau of Justice Assistance, may”.Pub. L. 109–271

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Director of the Bureau of Justice Assistance of the Office of Justice Programs may make grants to State and local prosecutors and law enforcement agencies in support of juvenile and young adult witness assistance programs.
to assess the needs of juvenile and young adult witnesses;
to develop appropriate program goals and objectives; and

children.label: A
children.text: counseling services to young witnesses dealing with trauma associated in witnessing a violent crime;

children.label: B
children.text: pre- and post-trial assistance for the youth and their family;

children.label: C
children.text: providing education services if the child is removed from or changes their school for safety concerns;

children.label: D
children.text: protective services for young witnesses and their families when a serious threat of harm from the perpetrators or their associates is made; and

children.label: E
children.text: community outreach and school-based initiatives that stimulate and maintain public awareness and support.
The term “juvenile” means an individual who is age 17 or younger.
The term “young adult” means an individual who is age 21 or younger but not a juvenile.
The term “State” includes the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, American Samoa, Guam, and the Northern Mariana Islands.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: d Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $3,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2006 through 2009.
